mnemonics: Mini Batches Balance. Mnemonic: My Big Brain Computes Faster, Not Slower. (M - Mini batch, B - Balances noise and compute, B - Best of both SGD and Batch, C - Computationally efficient, F - Faster convergence, N - Not too noisy, S - Samples a subset)
keyPoints: Uses a small, random subset (mini-batch) of the training data to compute an estimate of the gradient.
Strikes a balance between the computational efficiency of Stochastic GD and the stability of Batch GD.
Requires tuning the batch size as a new hyperparameter to optimize performance.
commonMistakes: Using a batch size that is too large, which loses the computational speed-up and behaves like slow Batch GD.
Using a batch size that is too small, which introduces too much noise (variance) and leads to unstable convergence.
